recommendations.message: Here are some cultural highlights near Kyoto that match your interests in Buddhist temples, monasteries, and free tours. Each spot includes a brief description, travel time from your base, and any reservation notes.

recommendations.name: 筒石観音堂
recommendations.description: A historic Buddhist temple located in the Kubiki region, offering serene worship spaces and traditional architecture.

recommendations.name: 浄興寺
recommendations.description: A tranquil temple in Joetsu featuring beautiful gardens and a peaceful meditation hall.

recommendations.name: 正光寺
recommendations.description: An ancient temple known for its intricate wooden carvings and seasonal garden displays.

recommendations.name: 専称寺
recommendations.description: A modest monastery offering quiet contemplation areas and a small tea garden.

recommendations.question: Which of these cultural sites would you like to keep, or would you like more options similar to these? You can also approve this category to move on to the next one.

response.prompt: Reply with a JSON feedback object: 'selected' = names of the 'culture' activities to keep, 'approve' = category done, 'finish' = build the itinerary now, 'quit' = end without one.

recommendations.message: Here are additional Buddhist temples and monasteries that share the quiet, historic vibe of 筒石観音堂 and 浄興寺. All are within about a 4‑hour drive from Kyoto and fit your interest in peaceful cultural sites.

recommendations.name: 正光寺
recommendations.description: A historic temple in Joetsu with beautifully preserved wooden structures and a tranquil garden, offering a reflective atmosphere similar to 浄興寺.

recommendations.name: 専称寺
recommendations.description: A modest monastery nestled in the countryside, featuring a simple meditation hall and a small tea garden that invites quiet contemplation.

recommendations.name: 西本願寺（Nishihonganji）
recommendations.description: One of Kyoto’s major Zen temples, known for its expansive grounds, historic buildings, and seasonal garden displays. A larger site but still offers serene corners.

recommendations.name: 東本願寺（Higashihonganji）
recommendations.description: Adjacent to Nishihonganji, this temple complex provides quiet inner halls and a beautiful pond garden, perfect for a reflective walk.

recommendations.name: 清水寺（Kiyomizu‑dera）
recommendations.description: Famous for its wooden stage and city views, the temple’s lesser‑known back‑garden offers a peaceful spot away from the crowds.

recommendations.name: 龍安寺（Ryoan‑ji）
recommendations.description: Renowned for its rock garden, the surrounding temple grounds provide a calm setting for meditation and quiet strolling.

recommendations.name: 大徳寺（Daitoku‑ji）
recommendations.description: A large Zen complex with multiple sub‑temples, many of which host free tea‑ceremony experiences and quiet meditation halls.

recommendations.name: 高野山（Koyasan）日帰りツアー
recommendations.description: A day‑trip to the sacred mountain monastery complex, featuring Okunoin cemetery, Kongōbu‑ji temple, and opportunities for meditation.

recommendations.question: Which of these additional sites would you like to keep, or would you like even more options? When you’re ready, we can move on to the next category.

response.prompt: Reply with a JSON feedback object: 'selected' = names of the 'culture' activities to keep, 'approve' = category done, 'finish' = build the itinerary now, 'quit' = end without one.

recommendations.message: Here are nature‑focused spots near Kyoto (and a bit farther in Niigata) that match your interest in gardens, parks, and scenic viewpoints. All are within a 4‑hour drive from your base.

recommendations.name: 新井総合公園
recommendations.description: A spacious public park in Myoko with walking trails, a pond, and seasonal flower beds – great for a relaxed stroll.

recommendations.name: 学校町2号公園
recommendations.description: A local park in Myoko offering open lawns, a small creek, and shaded benches – ideal for a picnic.

recommendations.name: 市民の広場
recommendations.description: Community square in Myoko with landscaped gardens, a fountain, and easy walking paths.

recommendations.name: 高田城址公園芝生広場
recommendations.description: Historic castle‑site park in Joetsu featuring grassy fields, stone walls, and panoramic views of the surrounding hills.

recommendations.name: わんパーク桜町
recommendations.description: A small park in Joetsu known for its cherry‑blossom trees in spring and a gentle walking loop.

recommendations.name: 春日山公園
recommendations.description: Hilltop park offering sweeping views of Joetsu city and surrounding mountains – perfect for a sunrise or sunset visit.

recommendations.name: Joshin‑Etsu Expressway Scenic Spot
recommendations.description: Designated pull‑over area with panoramic vistas of the Niigata landscape, ideal for a quick photo stop.

recommendations.question: Which of these nature spots would you like to keep, or would you like more options similar to these? When ready, we can move on to the next category.

response.prompt: Reply with a JSON feedback object: 'selected' = names of the 'nature' activities to keep, 'approve' = category done, 'finish' = build the itinerary now, 'quit' = end without one.

recommendations.message: Here are more parks and open‑space spots near Kyoto (in the Myōkō / Joetsu area) that share the relaxed, family‑friendly vibe of 新井総合公園 and 学校町2号公園.

recommendations.name: 市民の広場
recommendations.description: A community park in Myōkō with landscaped gardens, a small creek, and plenty of benches – perfect for a leisurely walk or picnic.

recommendations.name: 高田城址公園芝生広場
recommendations.description: Historic castle‑site park in Joetsu featuring open grassy fields, stone walls, and panoramic hilltop views.

recommendations.name: わんパーク桜町
recommendations.description: A small, tranquil park in Joetsu known for its cherry‑blossom trees in spring and a gentle walking loop.

recommendations.name: 春日山公園
recommendations.description: Hilltop park offering sweeping views of Joetsu city and surrounding mountains – ideal for sunrise or sunset visits.

recommendations.name: Myoko Nature Trail (Myoko‑Sanroku Trail)
recommendations.description: A short, easy walking trail near Myōkō town that winds through forested hills, with seasonal wildflowers and picnic spots.

recommendations.name: Joetsu Riverside Park
recommendations.description: A riverside green space along the Shinano River with walking paths, shaded areas, and river‑view benches.

recommendations.question: Which of these nature spots would you like to keep, or would you like even more options? When you’re ready, we can move on to the food category.

response.prompt: Reply with a JSON feedback object: 'selected' = names of the 'nature' activities to keep, 'approve' = category done, 'finish' = build the itinerary now, 'quit' = end without one.

recommendations.message: Here are vegetarian‑friendly dining options near Kyoto (including a few in the Joetsu area) that match your interests in restaurants and cafés. All are within a 4‑hour drive from your base and fit your vegetarian diet.

recommendations.name: えほん
recommendations.description: Cozy café in Itoigawa serving a variety of vegetarian Japanese set meals and specialty teas. Known for its calm atmosphere and seasonal menu.

recommendations.name: 海のだいどこや
recommendations.description: Seafood‑focused restaurant in Joetsu that offers a dedicated vegetarian kaiseki tasting menu using local vegetables and tofu. Reservations recommended.

recommendations.name: つるこし
recommendations.description: Chic café in Joetsu serving vegetarian ramen, rice bowls, and Japanese‑style desserts. Popular with locals; seating fills up quickly.

recommendations.name: Sakua de la noche
recommendations.description: Fusion café in Joetsu offering vegetarian tapas, craft coffee, and a relaxed evening vibe. No reservation needed.

recommendations.question: Which of these food venues would you like to keep, or would you like more suggestions similar to these? When you’re ready, we’ll finalize your activity itinerary.

response.prompt: Reply with a JSON feedback object: 'selected' = names of the 'food' activities to keep, 'approve' = category done, 'finish' = build the itinerary now, 'quit' = end without one.

recommendations.message: Here are a few more vegetarian‑friendly restaurants and cafés near Kyoto (including additional options in Joetsu) that match the style of えほん and 海のだいどこや.

recommendations.name: つるこし
recommendations.description: Cozy Joetsu café known for its vegetarian ramen, tofu‑based rice bowls, and Japanese‑style desserts. Popular with locals; reservations recommended for dinner.

recommendations.name: Sakua de la noche
recommendations.description: Fusion‑style café in Joetsu offering vegetarian tapas, craft coffee, and a relaxed evening atmosphere. No reservation needed.

recommendations.name: Mumokuteki Café
recommendations.description: Vegetarian café in central Kyoto serving seasonal set meals, matcha sweets, and a quiet garden seating area. Walk‑up only; no reservation required.

recommendations.name: Veg Out Kyoto
recommendations.description: Modern vegetarian restaurant near the Philosopher’s Path, offering kaiseki‑style multi‑course meals using local vegetables and tofu. Reservations recommended for dinner.

recommendations.question: Which of these new food venues would you like to keep, or would you like even more suggestions? When you’re ready, we’ll finalize your activity list.

response.prompt: Reply with a JSON feedback object: 'selected' = names of the 'food' activities to keep, 'approve' = category done, 'finish' = build the itinerary now, 'quit' = end without one.

itinerary.summary: A balanced 7‑day itinerary that centers on the nine confirmed activities – three cultural sites, three nature parks, and three vegetarian restaurants – all reachable within a 4‑hour drive from Kyoto. Two dedicated day‑trips cover the Niigata region, while the remaining days give you relaxed time in Kyoto to enjoy vegetarian meals and explore at your own pace.

morning: Arrive at Kansai International Airport, take the JR Haruka to Kyoto Station
Check‑in at your accommodation (handled by the lodging agent)
afternoon: Leisurely walk around the historic Higashiyama district – optional free self‑guided stroll
evening: Vegetarian kaiseki dinner at Kikunoi H (reservation recommended; tip‑based) – aligns with your interest in kaiseki
meals: Breakfast: Hotel breakfast (included)
Lunch: Café Bibliothèque (vegetarian menu, backup: Veg Out Kyoto)
Dinner: Kikunoi H – vegetarian kaiseki (reserve in advance)

days.theme: Myoko nature parks & Itoigawa cultural site
afternoon: Lunch at えほん (vegetarian, no reservation needed) in Itoigawa
Visit 筒石観音堂 (culture) – short walk around the temple grounds
evening: Dinner at 海のだいどこや in Joetsu (reservation required; enjoy seaside vegetarian dishes)
Return to Kyoto (≈3 h 30 min drive)
meals: Breakfast: Hotel breakfast
Lunch: えほん – vegetarian set (backup: local noodle shop with veggie options)
Dinner: 海のだいどこや – reserve via provided link
days.driving_notes: Total round‑trip driving ≈7 hours. Depart Kyoto by 07:00 am, aim to be back by 21:00 pm. Ensure you have a GPS or map app for rural roads.

afternoon: Free time to explore Joetsu town centre or relax at a café
evening: Dinner at つるこし (reservation required; vegetarian izakaya style)
meals: Breakfast: Hotel breakfast
Lunch: Veggie House Joetsu (vegetarian café, backup: convenience store bento with veggie options)
Dinner: つるこし – reserve via provided link
days.driving_notes: Round‑trip driving ≈6 hours. Leave Kyoto by 07:30 am, return by 20:30 pm.

morning: Free morning – optional stroll along the Kamo River
afternoon: Free afternoon – browse local shops or rest at your lodging
evening: Vegetarian dinner at Mumokuteki Café (backup: Veg Out Kyoto)
meals: Breakfast: Hotel breakfast
Lunch: Café Bibliothèque (vegetarian) or any local café
Dinner: Mumokuteki Café – vegetarian set menu

morning: Free morning – optional visit to Nishiki Market for vegetarian snacks
afternoon: Free afternoon – optional tea ceremony at a local temple (self‑guided, no fee)
evening: Vegetarian dinner at Aji no Kuni (backup: Veggie House Kyoto)
meals: Breakfast: Hotel breakfast
Lunch: Veg Out Kyoto – vegetarian bowls
Dinner: Aji no Kuni – vegetarian kaiseki (no reservation needed)

morning: Free morning – pack, enjoy a leisurely walk in a nearby park
afternoon: Free afternoon – optional museum visit or rest
evening: Vegetarian dinner at Kanga-an (backup: Café Bibliothèque)
meals: Breakfast: Hotel breakfast
Lunch: Café Bibliothèque or any vegetarian café
Dinner: Kanga-an – vegetarian shojin ryori (no reservation needed)

morning: Last‑minute souvenir shopping in Kyoto Station area
afternoon: Lunch at Veg Out Kyoto (vegetarian) before heading to the airport
meals: Breakfast: Hotel breakfast
Lunch: Veg Out Kyoto – vegetarian set
Dinner: On‑board flight meal (request vegetarian option)

day_trips: Myoko & Itoigawa nature and cultural day trip (April 4)

budget.notes: All listed activities are free or low‑cost; major expenses are vegetarian meals (including reservations for 海のだいどこや and つるこし) and car rental/fuel for the two day‑trips. Buffer covers souvenirs and any unexpected fees.

tips: Reserve 海のだいどこや and つるこし at least 3 days in advance using the provided booking URLs.
For the long drives, rent a compact car with GPS; tolls are minimal but keep cash for rural roads.
April is cherry‑blossom season – bring a light jacket and an umbrella.
Kyoto city buses accept the 1‑day pass (¥900 ≈ $6.5); it’s the cheapest way to move around on non‑driving days.
All selected restaurants offer vegetarian (shojin) menus; confirm any vegan needs when you book.
